jobs.title: Manager, Consulting – East
jobs.jd_full:
For over 20 years, Smartsheet has helped people and teams achieve–well, anything. From seamless work management to smart, scalable solutions, we’ve always worked with flow. We’re building tools that empower teams to automate the manual, uncover insights, and scale smarter. But more than that, we’re creating space– space to think big, take action, and unlock the kind of work that truly matters. Because when challenge meets purpose, and passion turns into progress, that’s magic at work, and it’s what we show up for everyday.

Smartsheet is seeking a forward-thinking Consulting Manager to lead a regional delivery team through a fundamental shift from traditional, effort-based implementation to a modern, value-driven advisory model centered around AI. In this role, you will lead the AI transformation, bridging the gap between technical execution and executive-level business consulting to ensure our customers achieve measurable and fast business value.

Operational management and active business development are the foundations of this role. This leader will institutionalize scalable, repeatable delivery processes to drive operational excellence and consistent customer outcomes, while serving as a critical strategic partner to account teams, fueling expansion. Layered on top is the leadership of a team of strategic advisors who are redefining their value as AI reshapes the professional services delivery. 

We are looking for a leader who balances a get-it-done operational mindset with a restless curiosity for what’s next. You should be a high-empathy coach who thrives in a transparent, win-as-one environment, with the resilience to lead a team through rapid change while maintaining a relentless focus on our customers' success.

You Will: 

  • Architect the Advisory Team: Interview, hire, and develop consultants with the requisite skills for the Advisory Era—prioritizing strategic work, stakeholder alignment, AI competencies, and an adoption-focused mindset.
  • Coach for Resilience: Foster an environment of daily experimentation, encouraging your team to test AI solutions, share insights, and adapt to evolving delivery approaches while staying focused on customer value.
  • Drive Go-To-Market (GTM) Excellence: Proactively partner with Sales and other GTM leaders to drive regional services bookings, proactively training partners to sell your team's unique expertise as well as supporting strategic account expansion.
  • Voice of the Customer: Engage directly with customers to understand their evolving needs, using those insights to suggest future service offerings and train your team to deliver against them. You will also maintain a billable target for strategic project involvement and serve as the first line of defense for customer escalations.
  • Drive Operational Excellence: Assume full accountability for your team’s fiscal health, ensuring the team meets or exceeds performance targets (KPIs) while maintaining consistent data accuracy in PSA tool(s) and every team member has visibility into their performance.
  • Lead Your Team Through the AI Pivot: Operationalize the transition to an AI-Native PS model, modernizing tools and methodologies to shift your team’s focus toward higher-value strategic activities.
  • Strategic Prioritization & Industry Alignment: Translate company objectives and shifting industry trends into clear, actionable priorities for your team, ensuring their activities remain focused on high-impact work as we navigate ongoing changes within the business and industry at large.
  • Intentional Resource & Lifecycle Management: Partner with the Resource Management Office (RMO) to balance workloads by matching consultant skills and capacity to project opportunities.
  • Other duties as assigned.

You Have: 

  • 7+ years of experience in SaaS Professional Services, with a proven track record of navigating business pivots or disruptive technology shifts.
  • Ability to formulate positions on industry trends and anticipate how technology developments—specifically AI—will impact customer needs.
  • 2+ years leading high performing teams of individual contributors, either in a formal people leadership role or as a project manager.
  • Work experience designing, selling, and delivering solutions in a SaaS Professional Services environment, preferably with team lead/people management responsibilities
  • High emotional intelligence and change leadership
  • Excellent client management skills and the ability to work with customers in both pre- and post-sales environments 
  • Proven track record of building influential relationships with GTM partners such as sales and customer success
  • Proficiency in using frameworks to develop insights into customer organizations and coaching team members on sophisticated problem-solving techniques
  • Experience applying change management, business process optimization, and AI strategies
  • Willing to travel periodically (up to 40%) based on customer and business need
  • Bachelor’s degree or equivalent combination of relevant work experience and education (minimum 7 years)

Current US Perks & Benefits:

  • Employer subsidized medical/vision and dental coverage for full-time employees
  • 401k Match to help you save for your future (50% of your contribution up to the first 6% of your eligible pay)
  • Monthly stipend to support your work and productivity
  • Flexible Time Away Program, plus Sick Time Off
  • US employees are automatically covered under Smartsheet-sponsored life insurance, short-term, and long-term disability plans
  • US employees receive 12 paid holidays per year
  • Up to 24 weeks of Parental Leave
  • Personal paid Volunteer Day to support our community
  • Opportunities for professional growth and development including access to Udemy online courses
  • Company Funded Perks, including a counseling membership, local retail discounts, and your own personal Smartsheet account
  • Teleworking options from any registered location in the U.S. (role specific)

Smartsheet provides a competitive base salary range for roles that may be hired in different geographic areas we are licensed to operate our business from. Actual compensation is determined by several factors including, but not limited to, level of professional, educational experience, skills, and specific candidate location. In addition, this role will be eligible for a market competitive incentive opportunity.

US Base Salary Pay Range
$122,500—$160,000 USD

Get to Know Us:

At Smartsheet, your ideas are heard, your potential is supported, and your contributions have real impact. You’ll have the freedom to explore, push boundaries, and grow beyond your role. We welcome diverse perspectives and nontraditional paths—because we know that impact comes from individuals who care deeply and challenge thoughtfully. When you’re doing work that stretches you, excites you, and connects you to something bigger, that’s magic at work. Let’s build what’s next, together.

Equal Opportunity Employer:

Smartsheet is an Equal Opportunity (EEO) employer committed to fostering an inclusive environment with the best employees. It is our policy to provide equal employment opportunities to all qualified applicants in accordance with applicable laws in the US, UK, Australia, Germany, Costa Rica, Japan, Bulgaria, and India. All qualified applicants will receive consideration without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, age, protected veteran or disabled status, or genetic information. 

If there are preparations we can make to help ensure you have a comfortable and positive interview experience, please let us know.

#LI-Remote

jobs.title: Translation Project Manager - US hours
jobs.jd_full:
As a Project Manager in our translations department, you will be responsible for managing several language-related projects simultaneously, ensuring that they are delivered on time, meeting quality expectations and within scope. With effective written and verbal communication, you will help ensure the teams are clear on the scope and expectations of clients and that the clients are kept fully informed as to the progress and status of their projects. You will work closely with our project management team, language professionals, to seek guidance when required and ensure project success.

  • Support in planning, execution, and monitoring several language-related projects simultaneously using project management tools to ensure that projects are delivered on time and within scope.
  • Support in maintaining and creating project schedules, and timelines ensuring that they are in line with SLAs and achievable and that they reflect the clients' requirements and expectations.
  • Monitor project progress on progress, challenges and/or successes throughout the project
  • Support in maintaining project documentation, including project plans, reports, and communications, using document management software such as SharePoint, etc., to ensure that we have accurate records of our projects.
  • Prepare quotes and invoices using project management software and accounting software to ensure that we generate revenue and meet our business goals.
  • Ensure adherence to company standards and procedures in project management to maintain our reputation for delivering high-quality language services.
  • Identify errors and areas of improvement and report when needed to the relevant stakeholders.
  • Support during meetings, documenting these when required and distributing minutes/actions to the team.
  • Bachelor's degree in a relevant field such as business, management, or language studies.
  • 2 or more years of experience in managing localisation projects.
  • Essential fluency in English. an additional language would be advantageous.
  • Excellent organizational and time management skills, with the ability to prioritize tasks and work under pressure to meet our clients' needs and achieve our business objectives.

Note: this will be an independent contractor position

Please submit your CV in English

About M3: A Japanese global leader in the provision of ground-breaking and innovative technological and research solutions to the healthcare industry. The M3 Group operates in the US, Asia, and Europe with over 5.8 million physician members globally via its physician websites which include mdlinx.com, m3.com, research.m3.com, Doctors.net.uk, medigate.net, and medlive.cn. M3 Inc. is a publicly traded company on the Tokyo Stock Exchange (jp:2413, NIKKEI 225) with subsidiaries in major markets including the US, UK, Japan, South Korea, and China, and in 2020 was ranked in Forbes’ Global 2000 list. The M3 Group provides services to healthcare and the life science industry. In addition to market research, these services include medical education, ethical drug promotion, clinical development, job recruitment, and clinic appointment services. M3 has offices in Japan, UK, France, Germany, Brazil, Sweden, China, USA, and South Korea, as well as India.

About the Business Division:

This role is part of MED PLANET, a Lyon based fieldwork agency and a wholly owned subsidiary of M3 Inc. MED PLANET is a leading provider of data-collection services for the pharmaceutical and healthcare industry. We specialize in conducting both qualitative and quantitative market research in France. MED PLANET provides high quality translations and transcription for healthcare fieldwork projects for the M3MR group.

Originally posted on Himalayas

jobs.title: Product Manager (Trust & Safety)
jobs.jd_full:
About Donorbox

Donorbox is a leading fundraising platform and donor management system for nonprofit organizations. Our mission is to accelerate positive impact worldwide by helping nonprofits become highly effective at raising funds and managing their supporter base. Since 2014, we have powered more than 100,000 global organizations to close to $3B in donations. 🚀

Our fast-growing company is profitable and bootstrapped with a healthy run rate. We have a fully distributed and diverse 150-person team based in 16+ states and 23+ countries.

🏅Donorbox is rated the #1 software for fundraising, donor management, and nonprofit payment on G2 based on hundreds of verified customer reviews. We’re proud of what we do, and our product reflects it!

The Role

Donorbox is passionate about empowering nonprofits to raise more funds through a smooth and secure giving experience. We are seeking a strategic and passionate Product Manager to join our growing team and lead the charge in evolving our Trust & Safety capabilities.

You will be the product leader responsible for protecting our system consumers—both the Organizations and their Supporters. Your mission is to provide organizations using Donorbox with a secure environment by mitigating fraud, managing risk, and maintaining content safety, all while ensuring an intuitive and frictionless experience for legitimate nonprofits. You will report to the Group Product Manager and work closely with Engineering, SRE, and Payments teams.

Responsibilities

  • Spearhead the product strategy for user protection, managing programmatic Org Verification, KYC processes, and Fraud Detection.
  • Secure the user journey by shipping product changes to our user onboarding to better capture verification data while maintaining a seamless activation flow.
  • Ship internal tooling to empower our support teams to quickly identify and take action on bad actors.
  • Configure monitors and alarms through vendors like Stripe Radar to proactively mitigate risk and decrease scammers.
  • Identify and mitigate link spam that degrades Donorbox’s domain authority and search ranking, including in AI-powered search tools and answer engines.
  • Recommend and collaborate with other Service and Product Line engineering teams on changes within their remit.
  • Work closely with Site Reliability Engineering (SRE) to improve WAF rules and platform monitoring.
  • Collaborate with the Payments team on refund and chargeback workflows to mitigate financial risk.
  • Manage platform integrity by overseeing policies around User Generated Content, Content Safety, Spam, and Terms of Service (TOS) violations.
  • Lead the verification badge product & program

Qualifications & Experience

  • 5+ years of product management experience, with proven experience within Trust & Safety, Fintech, or B2B SaaS platforms
  • Proven track record of successfully developing and launching risk mitigation, identity verification (KYC/KYB), or fraud detection features.
  • Working knowledge of GDPR and data protection principles, with practical experience implementing product compliance measures (full legal expertise is not required, but a strong compliance mindset is)
  • Working knowledge of SEO fundamentals and domain authority, particularly how platform-generated spam and low-quality links impact search reputation.
  • You have a hands-on, analytical mindset and enjoy diving into data to measure product impact and success.
  • Excellent communication and negotiation skills, capable of balancing technical debt discussions with stakeholder feature requests.
  • Passionate about the nonprofit sector and driven to make a positive impact.
  • A global mindset that understands the diverse needs of organizations worldwide.
  • Strong ability to analyze different business scenarios, user friction points, and risk metrics.

Details

  • Fully remote based in UK, France, Spain, Portugal

  • Salary range (by location):

    • UK: £64,000 – £75,000

    • France: €65,000 – €75,000

    • Spain: €55,000 – €65,000

    • Portugal: €50,000 – €60,000

  • Final compensation will depend on experience, skills, and location.

Benefits & Perks

  • Fully remote work from the comfort of your home
  • Eligibility for employee equity plan (stock options)
  • Reimbursement package for home office expenses and professional development, up to $1.5k
  • Generous time off policy of 21 days (birthday included 🎉), 8 holidays of your choice, and 2 paid volunteer days
  • Wellness program with fitness and mindfulness classes
  • Love your work and our mission of serving nonprofits!

The Application Process

We have 5 stages:

  1. Apply here and fill out our questions to tell us about you!
  2. Prescreen Call with the Talent Team
  3. Interview with Hiring Manager
  4. Panel/Final Interview
  5. Background & Reference Checks

If this sounds like the right role for you, please apply today and let us know why. We look forward to hearing from you!

jobs.title: Senior Product Manager - Operational Platform
jobs.jd_full:
Ilir Sela started Slice with the belief that local pizzerias deserve all of the advantages of major franchises without compromising their independence. Starting with his family’s pizzerias, we now empower over tens of thousands of restaurants with the technology, services, and collective power that owners need to better serve their digitally minded customers and build lasting businesses. We’re growing and adding more talent to help fulfill this valuable mission. That’s where you come in.

The Challenge to Solve

Slice is investing in the platform capabilities that help our products, teams and operations scale. As a Senior Product Manager for Core, you will define and deliver foundational capabilities that multiple teams and products rely on every day.  Some of these systems already exist and need to scale; others will be built from the ground up as we rethink how operational workflows should evolve in an AI-enabled environment.

We solve operational and platform challenges across operations, payments, data, support and internal product ecosystems.

Role

In this role, you’ll shape the internal tools and platform capabilities that power day-to-day operations across support, payments, and operational teams. You’ll identify workflow inefficiencies, prioritize opportunities, and partner with Development and Product Design to deliver intuitive products and scalable platform capabilities.

You’ll work across both existing systems that need to scale and new capabilities that need to be built from the ground up. The role requires strong systems thinking, comfort navigating ambiguity, and a practical approach to applying AI and automation to simplify workflows, reduce manual effort, and improve operational efficiency.

The Team

The individuals you’ll collaborate with are curious, motivated, and exceptionally proficient in their respective domains. They welcome challenging tasks and thrive on resolving them within an empowered and supportive setting.  Our fully remote team brings together talented people from across the US, Canada, UK and Eastern Europe, each contributing unique perspectives and expertise to drive success.

What We’re Looking For

We’re looking for a senior product manager who brings:

  • 5+ years of product management experience building and scaling operational platforms and internal tools.
  • Strong systems thinker with the ability to turn ambiguous problems into clear product strategy and execution.
  • Data-informed approach to product development using product and operational data to validate ideas, drive decisions and measure impact.
  • Strong understanding of how AI and automation can be applied to simplify operations, reduce friction, and improve team effectiveness at scale.
  • Interested in how AI and automation can reduce friction, simplify workflows  and improve operational efficiency at scale.
  • Experience translating user and operational needs into intuitive product experiences.
  • Strong product judgment with the ability to make difficult trade-offs and drive clear priorities.
  • Proven ability to communicate and collaborate effectively with product managers, product engineers, product designers and cross functional teams.
  • Excellent written and asynchronous communication skills for remote collaboration.
  • Familiarity with the pizza or restaurant industry is a plus.

The Extras

Working at Slice comes with a comprehensive set of benefits, but here are some of the highlights:

  • Access to medical, dental, and vision plans
  • Flexible working hours
  • Generous time off policies
  • Annual conference attendance and training/development budget
  • Market-leading maternity and paternity schemes
  • Discounts for local pizzerias (of course)

The Hiring Process

This process is expected to take 1–3 weeks in total.

  1. Application
  2. 30-minute introductory meeting
  3. 30-minute hiring manager meeting
  4. Take-home project and panel interviews
  5. Offer

Pizza brings people together. Slice is no different. We’re an Equal Opportunity Employer and embrace a diversity of backgrounds, cultures, and perspectives. We do not discriminate on the basis of race, colour, gender, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, religion, disability, national origin, protected veteran status, age, or any other status protected by applicable national, federal, state, or local law.

jobs.title: Product Manager, Fractional Independent Contractor
jobs.jd_full:
About aiEDU

The AI Education Project (aiEDU.org) is a 501©(3) nonprofit founded in 2019, dedicated to creating equitable learning experiences that empower students with AI literacy. We believe that everyone—especially those likely to be disproportionately impacted by AI systems—should have access to the knowledge and skills they need to thrive as workers, creators, consumers, and citizens. Our mission is to make AI literacy a fundamental part of every student’s education.

About the role

aiEDU is looking for two experienced product managers to support aiEDU in the development of educator- and student-facing products. These are short-term (6 month), fractional independent contractor positions, with the potential for extension based on future needs. We expect that product managers in this role will leverage AI to significantly speed up the product development process, and will function as player-coaches, upskilling our own team in effective product development along the way.

Project 1: Supporting our CEO and External team to build out vibecoding.aiedu.org. We envision this as a platform where educators, students, and partners can learn by building with AI. Over time, we anticipate this platform incorporating additional community-building elements like shared projects, monthly competitions, work showcases and community learning.

Project 2: Supporting our Programs and Learning teams to revamp their educator-focused products, including building an updated Curriculum Management System to replace our current Curriculum Portal, and defining and building new, partner-facing products to augment our program facilitation.

The right person will not just manage the product process—they’ll coach our internal teams through it, building PM capacity at aiEDU that outlasts the contract. You’ll work closely with our teams to understand current workflows, define requirements, and shepherd us through vendor evaluation or build decisions. Along the way, you’ll upskill our team on AI-assisted product development, AI augmented workflows and the shared infrastructure required to take our work to the next level.

⭐ Role details

  • Role type: This is an independent contractor role. No employer-employee relationship is created by this engagement.
  • Location: Fully remote. Some travel within the United States may be required; travel expenses will be reimbursed.
  • Compensation: This is a fractional independent contract role at up to 20 hours per week. Compensation will be $150 per hour, depending on experience.
  • Benefits: This is an independent contractor role and is not benefits-eligible.
  • Start date: As soon as possible.
  • Contract length: 6 months.

✅ Your Responsibilities

Lead the Product Discovery & Scoping Process

  • Define the product vision and roadmap in partnership with relevant aiEDU leaders and stakeholders
  • Define the problem space, surface constraints, and document current-state pain points
  • Conduct stakeholder interviews, user testing and other research to guide product development and feature prioritization
  • Translate requirements into a phased roadmap with clear milestones
  • Partner with our VP of Operations to identify and address relevant security, safety, privacy and legal considerations.
  • Develop and maintain a product requirements document (PRD) or equivalent

Build & Refine

  • Build the product end-to-end across a 2-3 month window, coordinating across aiEDU stakeholders to maintain alignment. Ideally, you can get the product to 90% yourself, and then partner with a contracted engineer to ensure the product is ready for full deployment.
  • Define evaluation criteria and product analytics aligned with aiEDU’s mission and operational needs; evaluate and refine product as needed.

Support aiEDU’s Internal Learning

  • Coach and upskill aiEDU’s own staff throughout the engagement, sharing best practices for product development and creating documentation, templates and frameworks that can be used for future product development.
  • Support our Operations team in advancing the aiEDU’s team internal AI readiness, advising as relevant on AI-assisted product development, AI augmented workflows and the shared infrastructure required to take our work to the next level.

🚀 About You

Required

  • 5+ years of product management experience, including experience leading 0→1. Experience turning ambiguous ideas into clear product requirements, prototypes, delivery plans, and launch decisions.
  • A hands-on AI power user, integrating AI into every stage of product development and evaluation.
  • Significant expertise in UI/UX front end development.
  • Technical fluency, including experience with authentication, data infrastructure and security and privacy considerations. Ability to define clear technical requirements and partner with contracted engineers to deliver.
  • Demonstrated ability to run discovery processes and user testing. Strong user empathy for educators, students, nonprofit teams, and partners.
  • Excellent verbal and written communication skills, including strong documentation habits.
  • Strong communication and facilitation skills—you can lead a cross-functional process without formal authority.
  • Genuine interest in mentorship; you’re comfortable explaining your process while doing it.

Preferred

  • Experience working in K-12 education, educator professional learning, curriculum or student-facing technology, including addressing privacy, safety and equity issues.
  • Familiarity with curriculum or learning operations workflows
  • Experience with tools commonly used in EdTech or content management (e.g., Clever, Google for Education) as well as Airtable

jobs.title: Associate II Content Publishing and Program Manager
jobs.jd_full:
The content publishing and program manager acts as the central coordinator of the content newsroom, overseeing content workflows, publishing timelines, flagship content production, and triage across campaign content streams. Reporting to the content operations lead, you will ensure seamless handoffs between content pods, content strategists, SMEs, operations and other stakeholders, optimizing processes for speed, quality and collaboration.

Responsibilities

  • Oversee end-to-end content workflows and manage timelines across multiple publishing streams.
  • Monitor and track project progress to ensure on-time, high-quality content delivery.
  • Coordinate dependencies across pods and content teams to maintain alignment.
  • Project manage the production of flagship assets — including Tech Radar, Looking Glass and other marquee reports, ensuring quality, timeliness and impact.
  • Triage campaign content intake requests and allocate assignments to writers and strategists based on priority, scope, and team capacity ensuring effective workflow management and resource alignment to key campaigns and programs for timely delivery.
  • Triage, manage and publish blog content for the corporate website and Medium, maintaining editorial standards.
  • Support the Content Ops lead by executing and tracking strategic publishing projects that help the content center scale via standardization of processes, templates, publishings/programs as well as consolidating and sharing best practices
  • Track and report on publishing pipeline health, capacity utilization, and delivery performance to support data-driven decision-making.
  • Streamline and optimize processes to improve efficiency and cross-team collaboration.
  • Ensure smooth handoffs between global and regional teams to support seamless execution.

Ideal candidate

We are looking for a highly organized, proactive and detail-oriented content operations professional who thrives in a fast-paced, multi-stakeholder environment.

Experience

  • 4+ years of experience in content operations, project management, editorial traffic management or newsroom coordination roles.
  • Experience working in a global B2B marketing or corporate communications environment.
  • Proven track record of managing project, complex content workflows across multiple stakeholders and regions.
  • Ability to effectively monitor, report, and communicate the status of strategic publishing projects to stakeholders.
  • Strong understanding of editorial workflows, content production lifecycles and digital publishing processes, CMS especially experience with AEM.
  • Experience supporting large, high-impact content programmes (e.g. flagship reports, campaigns or thought leadership series).

Skills & capabilities

  • Exceptional project management skills with the ability to manage multiple workstreams without losing attention to detail.
  • Confidence working with senior stakeholders and subject matter experts across regions and time zones.
  • Strong written communication skills and an editorial eye — able to review and sense-check content for clarity, consistency and brand alignment.
  • Ability to identify bottlenecks, improve processes and drive operational efficiency.
  • Working knowledge of JIRA for publishing workflow management and hands-on experience with AEM publishing are required.
  • Data-aware mindset — able to use metrics and reporting to track delivery and optimize workflows.
  • Prior experience in marketing operations or campaign program management
  • Experience in B2B marketing, preferably within digital transformation or technology consulting services.

Ways of working

  • Calm under pressure and solution-oriented.
  • Highly collaborative, with strong relationship-building skills.
  • Proactive in anticipating risks, managing dependencies and escalating appropriately.
  • Structured thinker who brings clarity and order to ambiguity.
  • Passionate about high-quality content and operational excellence.

Stakeholders

  • Content team (global director PR & editorial content, Tech and Business content pods, content ops team).
  • Field marketing, service line marketing and partner marketing.
  • Design traffic manager.
  • Global campaign strategy team.
  • Employer brand and alumni marketing.
  • Global Tech team and SMEs.

#LI-Remote

See here our AI policy.

jobs.title: Staff Technical Product Manager, Lead to Order
jobs.jd_full:
From Fivetran’s founding until now, our mission has remained the same: to make access to data as simple and reliable as electricity. With Fivetran, customer data arrives in their warehouses, canonical and ready to query, with no engineering or maintenance required. We’re proud that more organizations continue to leverage our technology every day to become truly data-driven.

About the Role

Fivetran is building data pipelines to power the modern data stack for thousands of companies.

We’re looking for an experienced Technical Product Manager to join the Systems Engineering team and own our Quote-to-Order (QTO) systems and processes. In this role, you’ll help shape how Fivetran launches products, manages pricing, generates quotes, and executes commercial agreements at scale.

You’ll partner closely with Sales, Product, Finance, RevOps, and Engineering to drive scalable solutions across product configuration, pricing, quoting workflows, approvals, and contract generation. A key focus of the role is enabling rapid go-to-market execution by improving how quickly new products, pricing models, and commercial structures can be introduced into our systems with high quality and operational consistency.

This role is ideal for someone who thrives in fast-moving environments, enjoys solving complex operational and systems challenges, and knows how to balance business needs, technical tradeoffs, and long-term scalability. You’re energized by bringing structure to ambiguity, asking thoughtful questions, partnering across teams, and improving the systems and processes that support how Fivetran sells and scales.

You’ll have the opportunity to directly influence Fivetran’s go-to-market agility, operational efficiency, and revenue growth while working closely with Product, GTM, Finance, and Engineering leaders across the company.

This is a full-time position based out of ourOakland office. Our hybrid work model offers a blend of remote flexibility and in-person collaboration, including two days in the office each week to connect and build as a team.

Technologies You’ll Use

Salesforce, Salesforce CPQ, IronClad, NetSuite, SQL, Looker, Lucidchart, Jira, Google Workspace, and other GTM, finance, and operational systems supporting Fivetran’s Quote-to-Order workflows.

What You’ll Do

Roadmap & Prioritization

  • Partner with GTM, Finance, Product, RevOps, and Engineering stakeholders to build and maintain the QTO systems roadmap.

  • Identify opportunities to improve operational efficiency, reduce friction, standardize workflows, and accelerate go-to-market execution.

  • Intake, triage, and assess inbound requests across pricing, quoting, contracting, and commercial operations workflows.

  • Evaluate business impact, operational complexity, technical feasibility, and scalability tradeoffs to help prioritize the highest-value initiatives.

Requirements & Process Design

  • Own requirements gathering and process design for prioritized QTO initiatives from discovery through delivery.

  • Translate ambiguous business needs into clear, actionable product and systems requirements.

  • Ask thoughtful, detailed questions to uncover edge cases, process gaps, operational risks, and scalability concerns.

  • Partner with business stakeholders to simplify and standardize workflows while balancing operational needs, user experience, governance, and system constraints.

  • Document business processes, system behaviors, data flows, decision logic, and acceptance criteria needed to support implementation.

Systems Delivery & Solution Design

  • Partner closely with Systems Engineering and technical teams to design scalable solutions across Salesforce CPQ, quoting, approvals, order management, contract workflows, and adjacent operational systems.

  • Help drive solution tradeoff discussions by balancing business impact, technical complexity, delivery timelines, and long-term maintainability.

  • Support delivery execution through backlog refinement, sprint collaboration, testing coordination, UAT support, rollout planning, and cross-functional enablement.

  • Ensure implemented solutions align to intended business processes and operational goals.

Product, Pricing & Commercial Systems

  • Own and evolve systems and workflows supporting product catalog management, pricing structures, packaging, quoting, discounting, approvals, and commercial governance.

  • Partner with Product, Finance, and GTM teams to operationalize new products, pricing models, and commercial motions quickly and accurately.

  • Drive scalable approaches to consumption-based pricing, quote standardization, approval governance, and commercial process automation.

  • Support operational readiness for product and pricing launches across systems, workflows, and enablement processes.

Stakeholder Management & Operational Excellence

  • Build strong cross-functional partnerships across GTM, Finance, Product, RevOps, Legal, and Engineering teams.

  • Provide clear communication, status updates, decision framing, and prioritization transparency across initiatives.

  • Define and track KPIs related to quoting efficiency, operational accuracy, launch readiness, adoption, and process health.

  • Measure and communicate the operational and business impact of delivered initiatives and identify opportunities for continuous improvement.

Metrics & Operational Excellence

  • Define and track KPIs related to quoting efficiency, operational accuracy, launch velocity, adoption, and process health.

  • Use data and operational insights to identify bottlenecks, prioritize improvements, and measure impact.

Skills We’re Looking For

  • 7+ years of experience in Technical Product Management, with significant exposure to Quote-to-Order systems.

  • Deep understanding of Quote-to-Order business processes within complex SaaS environments, including product configuration, consumption-based pricing, quoting, approvals, contract workflows, and order operations.

  • Hands-on experience with Salesforce CPQ, including product modeling, pricing logic, discounting, bundling, approvals, and templating

  • Familiarity with IronClad CLM for contract lifecycle management and NetSuite ERP for downstream financials.

  • Proven track record leading cross-functional QTO initiatives in SaaS or high-growth enterprise environments.

  • Strong technical foundation — familiarity with APIs, data flows, and system integrations.

  • Exceptional ability to translate business requirements into technical solutions and product roadmaps.

  • Excellent organizational and communication skills to manage multiple priorities and stakeholders.

Bonus Skills​

  • Salesforce certifications (CPQ Specialist, Administrator, Architect).

  • Experience with IronClad and Netsuite.

  • Experience working in public company environments with strong governance and process controls.

  • Background in automating and scaling workflows across multiple interconnected systems.

  • Background in software engineering (to better serve our Product integrations).

#LI-HYBRID#LI-AM1

The compensation range displayed on this job posting reflects the minimum and maximum target for new hire compensation for the target position and level, and may include sales incentives or target bonuses depending on the role.  Our compensation ranges are determined by role, level, and location. Our job titles may span more than one career level. Within the range, individual compensation is determined by additional factors, including job-related skills, experience, relevant education or training, business need, market demands. The compensation range is subject to change and may be modified in the future. Your recruiter can share more about the specific compensation range for your location during the hiring process.

Oakland Pay Range

$180,884—$226,105.50 USD

Perks and Benefits

  • 100% employer-paid medical insurance *
  • Generous paid time-off policy (PTO), plus paid sick time, inclusive parental leave policy, holidays, and volunteer days off
  • RSU stock grants*
  • Professional development and training opportunities
  • Company virtual happy hours, free food, and fun team-building activities
  • Monthly cell phone stipend
  • Access to an innovative mental health support platform that offers personalized care and resources in areas such as: therapy, coaching, and self-guided mindfulness exercises for all covered employees and their covered dependents.

* May vary by country and worker type - please reach out to your recruiter for more information

Click here to learn more about Fivetran’s Benefits by Region.

We’re honored to be valued at over $5.6 billion, but more importantly, we’re proud of our core values of Get Stuck In, Do the Right Thing, and One Team, One Dream. Read about us in Forbes.

Fivetran brings together high-quality talent across the globe to make data access as easy and reliable as electricity for our customers. We value and recognize that our customers benefit from having innovative teams made of people from many backgrounds, experiences, and identities. Fivetran promotes diversity, equity, inclusion & belonging through attracting, recruiting, developing, and retaining a diverse workforce, not only because it is the right thing to do, but because it helps us build a world-class company to better serve our customers, our people and our communities.

Your request and any information provided will be kept confidential and will not impact your candidacy. We look forward to hearing from you and accommodating your needs to the best of our ability.

jobs.title: Project Manager / Senior Project Manager (Construction)
jobs.jd_full:
Company Description

Turner & Townsend is a global professional services company with over 22,000 people in more than 60 countries.

Working with our clients across real estate, infrastructure, energy and natural resources, we transform together delivering outcomes that improve people’s lives. Working in partnership makes it possible to deliver the world’s most impactful projects and programmes as we turn challenge into opportunity and complexity into success.

Our capabilities include programme, project, cost, asset and commercial management, controls and performance, procurement and supply chain, net zero and digital solutions.

We are majority-owned by CBRE Group, Inc., the world’s largest commercial real estate services and investment firm, with our partners holding a significant minority interest. Turner & Townsend and CBRE work together to provide clients with the premier programme, project and cost management offering in markets around the world

Who are you?

You are an experienced Project Manager (3–5+ years) or Senior Project Manager (7+ years) with a strong background delivering institutional construction projects, ideally within post-secondary (university/college) environments. You thrive in complex, multi-stakeholder settings and bring strong leadership, organization, and client-facing skills.

You are detail-oriented, proactive, and confident in managing projects from feasibility through to closeout, with a proven ability to balance budget, schedule, and stakeholder expectations.

This role is based in Guelph, ON, with a strong on-site presence required. We welcome applications from candidates located in or near the Guelph area, as well as those willing to commute or relocate.

Job Description

As a Project Manager/Senior Project Manager, you will be involved in:

  • Leading projects through the full lifecycle: feasibility, design, procurement, construction, and closeout
  • Develop and manage budgets, schedules, risks, and reporting to ensure successful project delivery
  • Coordinate and collaborate with clients, consultants, contractors, and vendors
  • Lead project meetings, manage action items, documentation, and compliance standards
  • Oversee procurement, contracts, and consultant/contractor performance
  • Manage construction activities including RFIs, submittals, site visits, and payment approvals
  • Monitor and control change orders, risks, and issues, implementing mitigation strategies
  • Maintain strong client relationships and provide clear, data-driven updates and recommendations
  • Lead project closeout, including commissioning, turnover documentation, and lessons learned

Qualifications

  • Project Manager: 3–5+ years of experience in construction project management
  • Senior Project Manager: 7+ years of experience managing increasingly complex projects
  • Proven experience delivering institutional (university/college), commercial, or large-scale capital projects
  • Demonstrated experience in client-side project management environments
  • High attention to detail with strong documentation and reporting discipline
  • Bachelor’s degree in Engineering, Architecture, Construction Management, or related field
  • PMP designation (or working toward) is considered an asset
  • Primarily based on-site In Guelph, ON

Additional Information

What’s in it for you…

  • 100% covered health and dental benefits for you and your dependents (including paramedical services)

  • Health spending account, to top up expenses not covered in the benefits program

  • RRSP option with company matching

  • Annual learning and development budget

  • Access to a range of online learning tools, and support for career development and growth

  • Coverage of one professional membership or license fee per year, if directly related to your role

  • We believe in work-life balance, so you can recharge and take care of what matters most with vacation, flex and sick days

  • A flexible, hybrid work approach that allows colleagues to find balance between their professional and personal lives

  • Commitment to creating a diverse, equitable and inclusive culture that promotes a sense of belonging

The base salary range for the full-time Project Manager role is $70,000.00 - $110,000.00 per year and for the full-time Senior Project Manager role is $105,000 - $135,000 per year . Ranges are determined by role and level and represent a good faith effort to provide a fair and equitable salary. This range is a reflection of base salary only, not of a total compensation package. Please note Turner & Townsend reserves the right to pay more or less than the posted range, depending on candidate’s experience and qualifications.

Our inspired people share our vision and mission. We provide a great place to work, where each person has the opportunity and voice to affect change.

We want our people to succeed both in work and life. To support this we promote a healthy, productive and flexible working environment that respects work-life balance.

We are an Equal opportunity employer who will ensure accommodations are available for applicants with disabilities upon request. We celebrate diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees and actively encourage applications from all sectors of the community.

Please find out more about us at www.turnerandtownsend.com/

#LI-HS1

jobs.title: Senior Product Manager (Career Sites)
jobs.jd_full:
Company Description

SmartRecruiters is the Recruiting AI Company that transforms hiring for the world’s leading enterprises. Built for global scale, SmartRecruiters, an SAP company, delivers an AI-powered hiring platform that automates and optimizes the entire talent acquisition process, ensuring faster and smarter hiring decisions. More than 4,000 companies, including Amazon, Visa, and McDonald’s, rely on SmartRecruiters to build winning teams. In 2025, SmartRecruiters joined SAP, the global leader in enterprise applications. Together, SmartRecruiters and SAP are accelerating the reinvention of hiring by combining cutting-edge AI innovation with the scale, reach, and resources of SAP’s ecosystem.

At SmartRecruiters, we are a values-driven, globally focused tech company with strong financial backing and a bold vision for the future of work. We commit and dig deep, embracing challenges with grit, curiosity, and a drive for excellence. We foster a collaborative and inclusive work environment, where trust and determination bring us together. Because together, we will win.

Recognized by Fosway Industry Analysts as a strategic leader in recruitment technology for three consecutive years, and awarded by Comparably as a top company for Women, Perks and Benefits, Work-Life Balance, Happiness, Compensation, Diversity, and Culture - we take pride in creating a place where everyone can thrive. Our remote-friendly culture, competitive salaries, and strong internal mobility ensure that high performers have meaningful growth opportunities in an environment built on respect and empowerment.

Job Description

As a Senior Product Manager (Career Sites), you will work within a broader product team focused on building a world-class career site offering for global enterprises. Career sites are the primary entry point for candidates and a critical extension of our customers’ employer brand.

In this role, you will own and drive specific problem areas and initiatives within the career sites domain - such as job discovery, content management, performance, localization, integrations, or enterprise configuration - while contributing to a shared product vision and roadmap defined across multiple PMs.

You will collaborate closely with other Product Managers, Engineering, Design, and cross-functional partners to deliver scalable, high-quality experiences that meet both candidate expectations and enterprise requirements.

Work hours: this role is based on the US East Coast and requires starting the day around 8:00 AM ET to enable daily collaboration with our teams in Europe.

What you’ll do:

  • Own defined problem spaces and initiatives within the career sites product area
  • Contribute to a shared roadmap and product vision, working closely with other PMs in the domain
  • Lead discovery efforts to deeply understand candidate, customer, and market needs
  • Translate insights into clear requirements, priorities, and delivery plans
  • Partner with engineering and design to ship high-performing, enterprise-ready career site capabilities
  • Balance usability, performance, accessibility, SEO, localization, and compliance requirements
  • Collaborate with platform, AI, integrations, and SAP teams to ensure cohesive experiences across the suite
  • Define and track success metrics such as conversion, engagement, adoption, and performance

Why you’ll love working here:

  • Work on one of the most visible and business-critical spaces in hiring
  • Build products used by millions of candidates globally
  • Collaborate with strong product peers in a multi-PM, platform-oriented team
  • Combine SmartRecruiters’ product culture with the scale and ecosystem of SAP
  • Competitive compensation, benefits, and career growth opportunities

Qualifications

  • 6+ years of experience as a Product Manager in B2B SaaS or enterprise software
  • Experience building or owning career sites, job boards, candidate portals, or similar candidate-facing web products - or closely related domains such as employer branding platforms, ATS front-ends, or job search/discovery experiences
  • Strong product discovery skills and comfort working in shared ownership models
  • Ability to balance design quality with enterprise-scale constraints
  • Technical fluency and strong collaboration skills with engineering partners; familiarity with CMS architecture, SEO for job listings, or web performance is a plus
  • Clear, confident communication with cross-functional stakeholders
  • Experience with global, high-traffic products (localization, accessibility, compliance) is a plus

Additional Information

SmartRecruiters is proud to be an Equal Employment Opportunity employer. We do not discriminate based upon race, religion, color, national origin, gender (including pregnancy, childbirth, or related medical conditions), sexual orientation, gender identity, gender expression, age, status as a protected veteran, status as an individual with a disability, or other applicable legally protected characteristics

jobs.title: Project Manager ( Retail)
jobs.jd_full:
Company Description

At Turner & Townsend we’re passionate about making the difference. That means delivering better outcomes for our clients, helping our people to realize their potential, and doing our part to create a prosperous society.

Every day we help our major global clients deliver ambitious and highly technical projects, in over 130 countries worldwide.

Our team is dynamic, innovative and client-focused, supported by an inclusive and fun company culture. Our clients value our proactive approach, depth of expertise, integrity and the quality we deliver. As a result our people get to enjoy working on some of the most exciting projects in the world.

Job Description

  • Preparation and maintenance of project governance and administration documentation
  • Track project progress and provide regular reports on project status to project team and key stakeholders
  • To produce meeting minutes, agendas and monthly reports
  • Support design management and procurement activities.
  • Support stakeholder consultation
  • Communicate effectively with all project stakeholders
  • T&T safety on projects checklist to ensure compliance
  • To assist the Project Manager and Project Director with tasks such as; cost reporting, program, service provider performance monitoring, risk register updates, quality assurance etc
  • Tender research, collating potential bid work
  • Assistance with bids for future work in the region, including bid plans and liaison with the risk team.

Qualifications

  • A recognised Degree or equivalent qualification relating to a technical field: Construction Management or Project Management, Engineering, Architecture, Cost Management or relatable trade experience
  • 3- 5 years project management experience  (ideally client side/consulting)
  • Must be able to work within a large team environment and bring growth mindset.
  • Ability to thrive in a high pressure, fast paced environment.
  • Excellent command of written and spoken English with excellent report and writing skills
  • Must be able to be involved in general office assistance
  • Must be able to fit into a small team environment and bring a growth mindset
  • Retail project experience is ideal.

Additional Information

Our inspired people share our vision and mission. We provide a great place to work, where each person has the opportunity and voice to affect change. We want our people to succeed both in work and life. To support this we promote a healthy, productive and flexible working environment that respects work-life balance.

Turner & Townsend is an equal opportunity employer. We celebrate diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees and actively encourage applications from all sectors of the community.

Turner & Townsend does not accept any speculative or unsolicited CV’s that have been sent to our internal recruitment team or hiring managers from agencies outside of our preferred supplier list. Any speculative or unsolicited CV’s will be treated as a direct application.

jobs.title: Junior Business Analyst/Project Manager with French (She/ He/ They)
jobs.jd_full:
CAPCO POLAND

We are looking for a Poland-based candidate.

At Capco Poland, we’re not just another consultancy – we’re the spark behind digital transformation in the financial world. As a global leader in technology and management consulting, we help clients tackle complex challenges across banking, payments, capital markets, wealth, and asset management.

ROLE OVERVIEW

We are looking for an experienced Junior Business Analyst / Project Manager with French to support the delivery of strategic initiatives within the financial services sector.

In this hybrid role, you will combine business analysis expertise with project management responsibilities, working closely with business stakeholders, IT teams, and senior leadership to define requirements, manage delivery, and ensure successful project outcomes in an international environment.

KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

Business Analysis

  • Gather, analyze, and document business and functional requirements.
  • Facilitate workshops and stakeholder meetings.
  • Analyze business processes and identify improvement opportunities.
  • Translate business needs into clear functional specifications.
  • Maintain project and requirements documentation.

Project Management

  • Manage project plans, timelines, milestones, and deliverables.
  • Coordinate project activities across business and technology teams.
  • Track risks, issues, dependencies, and actions.
  • Monitor project progress and provide regular status reporting.
  • Support project governance and ensure delivery aligns with agreed objectives.

Stakeholder Management

  • Act as the key point of contact between business and IT stakeholders.
  • Facilitate communication and decision-making across project teams.
  • Manage stakeholder expectations and project communications.

KEY REQUIREMENTS

  • Fluency in French (C1+) and English.
  • Proven experience in a Business Analyst, Project Manager, or BA/PM role.
  • Experience delivering projects within banking, financial services, or consulting environments.
  • Strong requirements gathering and business analysis skills.
  • Experience managing project plans, risks, and stakeholder communication.
  • Knowledge of Agile and/or Waterfall delivery methodologies.
  • Proficiency with Jira, Confluence, and MS Office tools.
  • Strong analytical, communication, and organizational skills.

WHAT WE OFFER

We offer a flexible collaboration model based on a B2B contract, with the opportunity to work on diverse projects for leading financial institutions. Hybrid mode work from Kraków or Warsaw.

RECRUITMENT PROCESS

  • HR Interview with Recruiter
  • Skill Interview with Hiring Manager
  • Client Interview
  • Feedback and Offer

Joining Capco means joining an organization that is committed to an inclusive working environment where you’re encouraged to #BeYourselfAtWork.

#LI-HYBRID
